summary: Large gmregclear decompilation/matching PR for the game-mode regular clear/adventure area. It added substantial C bodies in src/melee/gm/gmregclear.c, recovered local structs and field layouts, expanded include dependencies, and updated gmregclear.h and gm/types.h prototypes/types. The bot reported +3292 matched-code bytes with 6 new matches and many gmregclear score improvements, while also reporting one broken function match and a .data regression. The author commented that regressions were caused by data locality and expected to be solved when linking.

smart_moves.text: The PR paired body recovery with header signature updates instead of leaving recovered functions behind UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gmregclear.c bodies added for gm_8017CE34/fn_8017D9C0/gm_8017DB88
raw_diff: gmregclear.h corresponding signatures made typed

smart_moves.text: The PR recovered small internal structs for repeated records rather than keeping the entire BSS/data state as opaque byte padding.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: RegClearSpawnEntry and RegClearCharEntry added
raw_diff: lbl_80472ED8 expanded into PlayerInitData, RegClearSpawnEntry array/pointers, and scalar fields

smart_moves.text: The implementation used ordinary C control flow for several recovered patterns, including switch statements and for/while loops, rather than only preserving placeholder blocks.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_8017CE34 uses switch(arg9), switch(arg1->x0.x9), and for loops over colors/player slots
raw_diff: fn_8017D9C0 uses while and for loops for sentinel scan and shuffle

decomp_lessons.text: When a recovered body proves callback signatures and field types, update the owning header/shared type definitions in the same scoped change so callsites use typed function pointers and real return types.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: UnkAdventureData x48/x50/x58 changed from UNK_T to typed function pointers
raw_diff: gmregclear.h function declarations changed from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed signatures

decomp_lessons.text: For gmregclear state, repeated fixed-size record access was made more reviewable by adding internal record structs and offset comments before using fields in new bodies.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: RegClearSpawnEntry and RegClearCharEntry added with offset comments
raw_diff: lbl_80472ED8 x54[101] and x6A4..x6B8 pointers added

decomp_lessons.text: Natural loops appear in the recovered code for sentinel scans, shuffling, color setup, and player-slot initialization; this PR is evidence that gmregclear matching can tolerate readable loops in some cases.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_8017D9C0 while loop scans until 0x21 and for loops shuffle/check candidates
raw_diff: gm_8017CE34 loops over three colors and fills remaining player slots

naming_conventions.text: Address-style names were retained for many uncertain functions/globals, while only locally supported record names such as RegClearSpawnEntry and RegClearCharEntry were introduced.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: functions remain named gm_8017CE34, fn_8017D9C0, gm_8017DB88 and globals remain lbl_80472D28/lbl_80472E48/lbl_80472ED8
raw_diff: typedef struct RegClearSpawnEntry and typedef struct RegClearCharEntry added

naming_conventions.text: Recovered but semantically unclear fields stayed as x-offset names and pad names rather than speculative semantic names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbl_80472D28_t uses x20, x24, x30, x48, x4C, x84, xC0 etc.
raw_diff: RegClearSpawnEntry fields named x0/x4/x5/x6/x7/x8/xC

notes: Strong evidence for file list, large gmregclear.c changes, header/type changes, bot-reported match/regression numbers, and the author data-locality comment.
The diff is large and the initially loaded diff excerpt was truncated, so this intake summarizes representative visible changes and targeted header/type reads rather than every added function body.
No reviewer comments were loaded; reviewer intent and acceptance rationale beyond merge metadata are not inferred.
The author comment attributes regressions to data locality, but the slice does not independently prove the cause or that linking later resolved it.
PAD_STACK usage is visible in the diff, but the slice does not include separate objdiff rationale for each PAD_STACK instance.

source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-level search fact, but should be curated against current source naming and organization before adding to path facts.

source_update_candidates.title: Treat unresolved data-locality regressions as explicit review risk
source_update_candidates.text: This PR had bot-reported data regressions and an author note that regressions were caused by data locality and expected to be solved when linking. A possible standard note is to record data-locality regression rationale separately from claimed text progress and verify later resolution when linking/split work changes.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists .data regression and one broken match
text_corpus: author comment says regressions are caused by data locality and will be solved when linking
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential refinement of verification/regression-ledger guidance; evidence is a single PR comment and should not be promoted without curator review or corroborating cases.

rejection_notes: Do not record that the .data regression was actually fixed by linking; the slice only contains the author's expectation.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale; no review comments or reviews are loaded.
Do not promote RegClearSpawnEntry/RegClearCharEntry field meanings beyond their names and offsets; most fields remain x-offset names.
Do not treat PAD_STACK usage here as generally accepted style without requiring narrow verifier evidence.
